commits: 4232349: docs(0.7.0): planning slice — revised-budgets + AC-020 ADR drafts + plan doc + slice skeletons

new_ac_proposals.title: Revised AC-012 budget (LIMIT-off) met on canonical runner at 1M corpus
new_ac_proposals.draft_budget: p50 ≤ 50 ms; p99 ≤ 200 ms (Option B of ADR-revised § AC-012)

new_ac_proposals.title: Revised AC-013 budget met on canonical runner (conditional on bulk-vec seed seam in 0.7.0)
Revised AC-019 budget met (conditional on AC-072)
Revised AC-020 envelope met after chosen architectural lever lands
Caller-visible top-K LIMIT argument honored on the search verb (conditional on Q4 = additive)
new_ac_proposals.draft_budget: behavioral; default unlimited; LIMIT-on perf-sub-budget p50 ≤ 25 ms; p99 ≤ 100 ms

pack7_trigger_snapshot.runner_spec: AMD EPYC 9V74 80-core (4 cores); Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS; glibc 2.39; SQLite 3.45.x via libsqlite3-sys 0.28.0; rustc 1.95.0; ubuntu-latest x86_64

axis_e_movement_decision: stays at 0.6.0 (perf-only does not move trait surface per Wake d-001)

verify.mkdocs_strict: green (Cleaning + Building; INFO note for release-notes/0.6.1.md not in nav is non-blocking)
verify.agent_verify: ok (exit 0; AC-037 BLOCKER on unshare -rUn is toolchain-missing env, not a regression)
verify.files_present: ok (plan doc + 2 ADR drafts + 11 slice skeletons + HANDOFF + planning prompt)

blockers_encountered: AC-037 toolchain blocker on rootless userns+netns; environmental, does not affect planning-slice deliverable; documented for orchestrator awareness

open_hitl_questions.options: omnibus single ADR | split (AC-012 ADR + parallel AC-013/019/020 successor ADRs)
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: split; AC-012 budget per Option B (p50 ≤ 50 ms; p99 ≤ 200 ms); AC-013/019 conditional on bulk-vec seed seam being feasible in 0.7.0; AC-020 mechanism owned by ADR-0.7.0-ac020-architectural-lever

open_hitl_questions.topic: AC-020 architectural lever (PCACHE2 / WAL2 / R-W split / vendor swap)
open_hitl_questions.options: PCACHE2 (smallest blast radius) | WAL2 (durability surface) | R-W split (architecture change) | vendor swap (last resort)
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: PCACHE2 default contingent on PERF-DIAG slice confirming H1 (page-cache mutex contention) as primary hypothesis; fallback ranking per ADR § Recommendation

open_hitl_questions.options: diagnostic-first (PERF-DIAG before any structural lever) | optimistic (PRAGMA + lever in parallel)
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: diagnostic-first per whitepaper § 7.1; stop-rule: if PERF-DIAG shows no super-linear-grower at canonical scale, close AC-020 by re-measurement without any lever landing

open_hitl_questions.topic: Caller-visible top-K LIMIT shape (additive vs breaking)
open_hitl_questions.options: additive (default unlimited, opt-in cap) | breaking (required arg)
open_hitl_questions.recommendation: additive; preserves 0.6.x caller-spec; semver-minor allows additive surface but should not silently break callers per ADR-0.6.0-no-shims-policy

next_step_for_orchestrator: codex reviewer pass on plan doc + ADR drafts per dev/design/orchestration.md § 3; HITL on Q1..Q4 before any PERF-* slice opens; ADR drafts flip to status: locked only after HITL closes the questions; do NOT spawn any PERF-* implementer until referenced ADRs read status: locked (grep -l 'status: locked' dev/adr/ADR-0.7.0-*.md).
